output:
Reviewing the branch for story `21-5-delete-workflow-machine-and-hierarchical-flow` now. I’m locating the implementation diff and the related tests first so the review is scoped to the actual story changes.I’ve confirmed the workspace already contains the story’s implementation as uncommitted changes on `master`, not a dedicated branch. Next I’m reading the story spec and the actual diffs for the workflow/parser/runner files to check the deletion and inlining work against the acceptance criteria.The main deletion landed, but I need to verify whether the behavior and coverage actually survived the move. I’m checking the parser implementation, the migrated tests, and the remaining import graph for any dead references or architectural breakage.I’ve already found one concrete architecture-policy regression in the new runner split, and I’m validating whether there are additional behavior or coverage gaps beyond that. Running the boundary check plus a few targeted searches now to turn that into a precise verdict.The migrated parser tests pass and the deleted-file/import checks are clean. I’m doing one more pass on buildability and on architecture constraints, because this story is supposed to finish the decomposition plan rather than just move symbols around.1. High: The story does not meet the test-suite acceptance criteria because the extracted runner introduced four bare `catch {}` blocks without the required `// IGNORE:` annotation in [workflow-runner.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-runner.ts#L19), [workflow-runner.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-runner.ts#L22), [workflow-runner.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-runner.ts#L39), and [workflow-runner.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-runner.ts#L42). `npx vitest run src/__tests__/boundaries.test.ts` fails on these exact lines, so AC2 and AC16 are not satisfied.

2. Medium: The inlining work leaves [workflow-parser.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-parser.ts#L1) at 656 lines, which violates the engine architecture’s file-size rule in [_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics-xstate-engine.md](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics-xstate-engine.md#L41) and [_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture-xstate-engine.md](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture-xstate-engine.md#L320). The story spec explicitly called out splitting again if the parser exceeded 300 lines in [_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/21-5-delete-workflow-machine-and-hierarchical-flow.md](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/21-5-delete-workflow-machine-and-hierarchical-flow.md#L187). That means the deletion landed, but the “complete the decomposition plan” goal is still not met.

I did not find a concrete security flaw in the reviewed diff.

AC coverage: AC1 passes (`npm run build` succeeded). AC3-13 and AC15 largely look satisfied from the diff and targeted checks: deleted files are gone, imports were migrated, `workflow-parser-hierarchical` tests pass, and no `from ...workflow-machine` / `from ...hierarchical-flow` imports remain in `src/`. AC14 appears satisfied for story-specific references because `tsc --noEmit` produced no errors mentioning those deleted modules, though there are unrelated TS errors in [workflow-machine.test.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/__tests__/workflow-machine.test.ts#L182). AC2 and AC16 fail for the boundary reason above. Separately, the story’s proof doc and exec-plan file are still missing.

<verdict>fail</verdict>
<issues>
- Boundary-policy regression: 4 bare catch blocks in `src/lib/workflow-runner.ts` break `src/__tests__/boundaries.test.ts`, so the full vitest suite does not pass.
- Architecture violation: `src/lib/workflow-parser.ts` is now 656 lines, exceeding the documented 300-line limit and the story’s own fallback-split instruction.
</issues>
